2605.01392.title: Using LLMs in Software Design: An Empirical Study of GitHub and A Practitioner Survey
2605.01392.paper_summary: Recent advancements in Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated significant potential across a wide range of software engineering tasks, including software design, an area traditionally regarded as highly dependent on human expertise and judgment. However, there has been little research focusing on how LLMs are used in software design, nor on the associated benefits and drawbacks. This paper aims to bridge this gap by empirically investigating how software developers utilize LLMs in the context of software design. We conduct a mixed-methods study, combining a mining study of 291 developer-ChatGPT conversations shared on GitHub with a survey of 65 software practitioners. Our findings reveal nine distinct categories of design tasks supported by ChatGPT, including architecture design, data model design, and the use of design patterns. We further characterize developer-ChatGPT interactions, showing that developers primarily use ChatGPT for knowledge acquisition and design-related code generation, with most tasks situated at the detailed design level. The study identifies seven key benefits of utilizing LLMs in software design as perceived by developers, such as better technology selection and the early detection of design flaws. We also uncover six limitations, including the generation of overly lengthy and difficult-to-read outputs, the creation of inexecutable or incorrect code, and a heavy reliance on context that can lead to hallucinated results. These findings provide an evidence-based characterization of current LLM use in software design from both open-source and practitioner perspectives, highlighting a tension between perceived benefits and limitations, which lays a foundation for future research and the development of effective techniques and tools to integrate LLMs into software design practices.
2605.01392.paper_summary_zh: 最近在大型語言模型（LLMs）方面的進展顯示出在廣泛的軟體工程任務中具有顯著潛力，包括軟體設計，這一領域傳統上被認為高度依賴人類專業知識和判斷。然而，針對LLMs在軟體設計中使用的研究仍然很少，對於相關的優勢和缺點也缺乏探討。本文旨在通過實證研究填補這一空白，調查軟體開發者在軟體設計背景下如何利用LLMs。我們進行了一項混合方法研究，結合了對291個在GitHub上分享的開發者與ChatGPT對話的挖掘研究，以及對65位軟體從業者的調查。我們的研究結果揭示了ChatGPT支持的九個不同設計任務類別，包括架構設計、數據模型設計和設計模式的使用。我們進一步描述了開發者與ChatGPT的互動，顯示開發者主要使用ChatGPT進行知識獲取和設計相關的代碼生成，大多數任務位於詳細設計層面。該研究確定了開發者認為在軟體設計中利用LLMs的七個主要優勢，例如更好的技術選擇和早期檢測設計缺陷。我們還發現了六個限制，包括生成過長且難以閱讀的輸出、創建不可執行或不正確的代碼，以及對上下文的高度依賴，這可能導致幻覺結果。這些發現從開源和實踐者的角度提供了對當前LLM在軟體設計中使用的基於證據的特徵描述，突顯了感知的優勢與限制之間的緊張關係，為未來的研究以及開發有效的技術和工具以將LLMs整合到軟體設計實踐中奠定了基礎。
2605.01392.author: Yifei Wang et.al.
2605.01392.authors: Yifei Wang, Ruiyin Li, Peng Liang, Yangxiao Cai, Zengyang Li, Mojtaba Shahin, Arif Ali Khan, Qiong Feng
